vocab.example: I relish the opportunity to command the vehicle because it gives me a sense of absolute control over the journey.

vocab.meaning: lái xe qua những địa hình khó khăn/hiểm trở
vocab.example: I prefer being the driver when we navigate arduous terrain, as I trust my own instincts more than anyone else's.
Being a passenger allows me to be a passive observer of the world outside the window, which I find quite relaxing.
In heavy city traffic, I'd rather be a passenger to curtail the stress associated with constant braking and accelerating.

vocab.meaning: sự tập trung/đắm chìm hoàn toàn
vocab.example: Driving requires total immersion in the task, which helps me disconnect from my work-related worries.

vocab.meaning: sự tập trung không gián đoạn
vocab.example: Safety on the road demands uninterrupted focus, so I take my responsibility as a driver very seriously.
I usually opt to be a passenger on long trips so I can recline in comfort and perhaps even take a nap.

vocab.meaning: cảm giác cực kỳ hưng phấn
vocab.example: There is a sense of sheer exhilaration when you're driving down an empty coastal road at sunset.
By taking turns as the driver, we can alleviate the burden of exhaustion during cross-country travels.

vocab.word: be at the beck and call of
vocab.ipa: /bi ət ðə bek ənd kɔːl əv/
vocab.meaning: phụ thuộc vào sự điều khiển/sắp xếp của ai đó
vocab.example: As a passenger, you are somewhat at the beck and call of the driver's schedule and their choice of route.
